title: Iran and US agree on communication mechanism for security in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The talks in Oman allow for the establishment of a direct contact network, while Japan considers the dispatch of its Self-Defense Forces.

title: Woman sentenced to 27 years in prison for murder of high school student in Asahikawa
summary: The court deemed the crime extremely cruel and an affront to human dignity; the reading of the sentence was interrupted by an intruder.

title: Japan's prime minister proposes reducing consumption tax on food to 1% for two years
summary: The measure, which aims to ease the cost of living, would be temporary and return to 8% after the trial period; the financing would not involve more public debt.

title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces intention to resign amid party crisis
summary: Starmer yields to internal pressures following a poor result in a by-election and a drop in his poll ratings.

title: Work advances to reform the Imperial House Law and ensure dynastic succession
summary: Political parties will be consulted on July 25 about the amendment bill, which seeks to increase the number of members of the imperial family.

title: Six people arrested for attempting to illegally import 49 kg of gold hidden in mannequins
summary: The shipment, valued at over 100 million yen in evaded taxes, was discovered during a customs inspection at a port.

title: Fair Trade Commission sanctions Kawai for paying extremely low wages to music teachers
summary: It is the first resolution of its kind in Japan against a company for obtaining services at unreasonably low prices.

title: Two schools in Kawasaki investigated for conducting medical exams with students half-naked
summary: The schools allegedly forced students to undress without justification, sparking protests from parents.

title: Tokyo police ban detective agencies from sharing information with stalkers
summary: This measure is applied for the first time, to prevent stalkers from obtaining personal data of their victims through private investigators.

title: Japan supports Ehime Prefecture over leak of 'Red Princess' mandarin seedlings
summary: The central government will provide assistance to investigate the possible illegal export of the patented variety to China.

title: 30 years after repeal of leprosy law, stigma and discrimination persist
summary: An official ceremony commemorated deceased patients and condemned the prejudice still faced by those affected.

title: Nikkei 225 index hits new all-time high, surpassing 72,000 points
summary: The sustained rise for six consecutive sessions is driven by the boom in technology and semiconductor stocks.

title: Cape Verde scores first goal in World Cup history and draws with Uruguay
summary: The smallest team in the tournament celebrated two goals against the sky blue, in a match that will go down in the history books.

title: Prime Minister Takichi calls for revision of vice-capital law to exclude voting in all of Osaka
summary: Governor Yoshimura confirmed that the prime minister supports the project but seeks changes in the referendum.

title: Three students die in school shooting in Philippines; two arrested
summary: The suspects, also students, were arrested in Batangas province after the armed attack.

title: North Korea installs barbed wire and prepares minefields near border with South Korea
summary: North Korean army violates armistice by reinforcing security in the Demilitarized Zone, according to Seoul.

title: Removal of radioactive debris begins at Fukushima Daiichi Unit 1 after 15 years
summary: Dismantling work proceeds slowly at the plant affected by the 2011 nuclear accident.

title: China bans export of dual-use items to ten US military organizations
summary: Beijing's measure responds to the inclusion of Chinese companies on Washington's defense blacklist.

title: Iconic 'Doctor Yellow' bullet train to be retired in January 2025
summary: The yellow shinkansen, used for track inspections, bids farewell after decades of service and as a good luck charm.

title: Critical bullying situation declared at Tokai Shuyo University rugby club
summary: A student suffered serious injuries after being strangled by teammates, forcing the institution to intervene.
